title: Prayer as Coping: Types and Boundaries
learningObjective: After this exercise, the user will understand different types of prayer from a psychology perspective and recognize when prayer supports wellbeing versus when it may delay needed help.
learningObjectiveAr: بعد هذا التمرين، سيفهم المستخدم أنواع الصلاة المختلفة من منظور نفسي.

content.scenario:
Research on prayer and mental health (Masters & Spielmans, 2007) shows:

**Positive forms:**
- Meditative/contemplative prayer → similar benefits to meditation (stress reduction)
- Gratitude-focused prayer → linked to wellbeing (Emmons & McCullough, 2003)
- Conversational prayer → sense of connection, emotional processing

**When prayer may NOT be enough alone:**
- When used to REPLACE professional help for clinical conditions
- When guilt from 'unanswered prayers' increases distress
- When prayer becomes compulsive (possible scrupulosity)

Key principle: Prayer AND professional help can coexist beautifully.
content.scenarioAr: البحث عن الصلاة والصحة النفسية يظهر أنواعاً مختلفة.

task.instructions: For each scenario, identify: prayer supporting wellbeing or prayer potentially delaying needed help?
task.instructionsAr: لكل سيناريو: الصلاة تدعم الرفاهية أو تؤخر المساعدة المطلوبة؟

items.text: A student prays for calm before exams and uses breathing techniques learned from prayer rituals. → Prayer supporting wellbeing
items.explanation: Positive use. The meditative aspects of prayer naturally support stress management. This complements healthy coping.

items.text: A student with severe depression says 'I just need to pray harder' and refuses to see a counselor. → Prayer potentially delaying help
items.explanation: Concerning pattern. While prayer can support recovery, using it to REPLACE professional help for clinical depression can delay critical treatment.

feedback.correct: Well balanced! The healthiest approach recognizes prayer as a valuable support that complements, not replaces, professional help when needed.
feedback.correctAr: متوازن! النهج الأصح يعترف بالصلاة كدعم قيم يكمل المساعدة المهنية.
feedback.incorrect: Review: the key is not whether prayer helps (it often does), but whether it's being used to AVOID professional help when needed.
feedback.incorrectAr: راجع: المفتاح ليس ما إذا كانت الصلاة تساعد، بل ما إذا كانت تُستخدم لتجنب المساعدة المهنية.

whatNotToDo: Don't dismiss the psychological benefits of prayer — research supports them
Don't use prayer as the ONLY response to clinical mental health conditions
evidence: Masters, K.S., & Spielmans, G.I. (2007). Prayer and health. Journal of Behavioral Medicine, 30(4), 329-338.
safetyNote: ⚠️ This is a psychology perspective. No theological claims are being made about the spiritual efficacy of prayer.
com_b_mechanism: Prayer type awareness builds reflective motivation for balanced coping
